column2: Do you agree to take part in this academic research questionnaire?
column4: What is your highest level of education?
column5: What best describes how you mainly use a computer or phone?
column6: Which of these have you used in the past year? (tick all that apply)
column7: How confident do you feel using online services and apps in general?
column8: How often do you experience slowness or problems when using online apps or services? For example: a website taking a long time to load, an app freezing, or being unable to access a file
column9: When an online service runs slowly or goes down, how much does it affect you?
column10: Have you ever had to wait longer than expected to access your files, emails, or apps at a busy time? For example: during a big software update, end of the work day, or a popular event
column11: What do you usually do when an online service is slow or unavailable?
column12: How important is it to you that the online services you use are fast and always available?
column13: Have you ever noticed an online service getting worse at a specific time of day or week? For example: slower in the morning, worse on Mondays, or during a big news event
column14: Do you think online services should be able to predict when they will get busy and prepare for it in advance?
column15: Would you be willing to pay slightly more for a service that guaranteed it would never slow down, even during very busy periods?
column16: How much do you trust that your online service provider manages their systems responsibly to keep things running smoothly?
column17: If an online service went down unexpectedly and you lost access for a few hours, what would be your most likely reaction?
column18: Have you ever received a warning that you are running out of online storage space? For example: "Your Google Drive is nearly full" or "iCloud storage is almost used up"
column19: When a service you use is slow, what do you think is usually the cause?
column20: How much do you think about the computing resources (servers, storage, processing power) that run the apps you use daily?
column21: Do you think technology companies waste computing resources by over-preparing for demand that never arrives?
column22: How important is it to you that the technology companies you rely on are using their computing resources efficiently and sustainably? For example: not running unnecessary servers that waste energy
column23: Would you find it useful if apps gave you an advance warning like "This service may be slower than usual between 5pm and 7pm today"?
column24: Overall, how satisfied are you with the reliability of the online services you use most?
column25: Is there anything else you'd like to share about your experience with online services â€” good or bad?
